methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $5.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: HistoSonics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.25B

private.name: Formation Bio
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series D), post-money $1.7B+

data.narrative:
Synchron, a Series D biotech company founded by Tom Oxley and Rahul Sharma, has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis employs two applicable methods against a peer universe of six private companies in the Biotech & Health sector at comparable or adjacent stages, supplemented by three public biotech comparables.

The Stage-Progression method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $200M raised baseline, implying a valuation of $300M. The Peer-Median method anchors to six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers—including HistoSonics ($2.2B at Series D), Formation Bio ($1.7B at Series D), Abridge ($5.3B at Series E), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B at Series C), and Insitro ($2.4B at Series C)—yielding a median valuation of $2.3B with a range of $1.5B to $5.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue for Synchron. The sector P/S median across public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) is 5.8x, but remains inapplicable without private-company revenue data.

Triangulation across the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $255M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate scaling relative to the peer cohort, particularly given heterogeneity in peer valuations and Synchron's stage positioning within that distribution.
